title: uragoga morae
intro: Uragoga morae, also known as the 'Indian squill', is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Uragoga morae has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying or powdering the roots, which are then used to make teas, infusions, or ointments for medicinal purposes.
